mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which compresses forward earnings estimates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of months where both RSAFS and HOUST decline, or joint Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain negative or flat in over 50% of INDPRO uptrend episodes, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC fails to exceed 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 7.0 percent year-over-year, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, elevating real discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 7.0%, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand, which directly lifts corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and flat payroll employment indicates structural labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending power, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth falls below 100k
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth falls below 100k, or the interaction Rank IC fails to exceed 0.04 in the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side bottlenecks and demand destruction, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of INDPRO decline episodes during rate hikes, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC drops below 0.02.
